meta.source: ylo-experiment/HANDOVER.md + blackboard-workflow-pattern.md + poem-os/poem/ + clients/supportsignal/prompt.supportsignal.com.au/ — synthesised from two deep-dive agent reports 2026-05-21
meta.canonical_chain: YLO probes (proven) → POEM OS docs (Oscar agent, poem-executor) → prompt.supportsignal (POEM install, new-incident.yaml)
meta.generator: Peter (Mochaccino dark-factory workspace) — three-way comparison view

thesis.headline: Same disease, three medicines
thesis.disease: An orchestrator that poisons itself by accumulating every step's payloads — transcripts, intermediate values, wrong attempts — until it summarises, editorialises, or fabricates instead of passing data through.

medicines.one_liner: Keep the LLM orchestrator, but take it out of the data path — workers are subagents with clean context that write the store directly and return one-line acks.
Keep the LLM orchestrator in the data path, and legislate good behaviour — ~600 lines of Oscar agent rules: no summarising, no augmentation, mechanical gate evaluation.
Delete the LLM from the orchestration seat entirely — compile the workflow to plain JavaScript that calls stateless provider.call(prompt, schema) per step.

subjects.name: prompt.supportsignal
subjects.tagline: A POEM install, compiled to JS
subjects.kind: client workspace · NDIS incident analysis · ~140 tests

lenses.label: Prompt Engineering
lenses.question: How are prompts authored, parameterised, versioned?
lenses.verdict: ~90% aligned — all three are .hbs descendants of AWB Gen 3

cells.subject: ylo
cells.detail: Output shape described in prose ("Return ONLY JSON: {...}"). No co-located schema (one exception). Informal, file-level versioning. Prompts lifted verbatim from AWB Gen 3.

cells.subject: poem
cells.detail: Schema carries placeholders{type,required,example}, outputSchema, version. Statically type-checked: {{var}} / {{var.prop}} / {{#each}} compatibility validated against the runtime value before any step runs.

cells.subject: supportsignal
cells.detail: Each prompt pairs a .hbs with a .json carrying modelType ("quick"/"analysis"), maxTokens, temperature, and outputSchema validated at runtime via SchemaValidator. Versioned + .old/ archive dirs + git.

lenses.label: Workflow Data Shape
lenses.question: How is the multi-step pipeline declared? What's the data shape?
lenses.verdict: Same DAG vocabulary, different serialisation — POEM has richer control flow

cells.subject: ylo
cells.detail: steps[]{ type, inputs[], stores, workerContract, humanGate, promptFile, outputKeyMap }. Five step types: transform / checkpoint / action / gate / elicit. Pure dataflow dependency. Gate type exists; conditional-parallel not first-class.

cells.subject: poem
cells.detail: steps[] with action: llm | llm-parallel | llm-conditional-parallel, gate checks[] with onPass/onFail routing, skipIf / condition (starts_with/contains/equals), active:false disable cascades. Materially richer branching than YLO.

cells.subject: supportsignal
cells.detail: Gate (invisible auto data-readiness) vs Checkpoint (visible human decision) categorically split. seed.mapping[] snake→camel, sections[].steps[], output.{drop,group} reshape. The production exemplar of the POEM shape.

lenses.label: Architecture · Runner · Isolation
lenses.question: What executes the workflow? How is state held? How is context isolated?
lenses.verdict: THE FORK — they diverge entirely here

cells.subject: ylo
cells.summary: Orchestrator IS an LLM · isolation is BEHAVIOURAL
cells.detail: The Claude Code session is the conductor. Isolation enforced by subagent spawning + ack-only logs — provable by grep (no payload in conductor log, no bytes in store). State = append-only EAV JSONL, projected last-write-wins.

cells.subject: poem
cells.detail: (1) render-only TS engine — no LLM call at all; (2) Oscar conversational agent — the LLM orchestrator that failed in field testing; (3) poem-executor CLI — compiled JS. State = one mutable complete.json (last-state-wins, history lost to a separate event log).

cells.subject: supportsignal
cells.summary: Orchestrator is COMPILED JS · isolation is STRUCTURAL & free
cells.detail: Compiler emits .compiled.js; run(incidentData, provider) calls provider.call(renderedPrompt, schema) per step. Each LLM touch is a stateless single-shot — no conversational context to poison. State = flat in-memory store, saved once at the end.

lineage.title: The Oscar lineage
beats: YLO's "Oscar" — the naive 'just run it in main context' failure the blackboard pattern fixes — IS literally POEM's Oscar agent.
POEM's docs never contain the words "context poisoning" (zero hits in the repo). That phrase is YLO's retro-label.
What POEM actually documented (Oscar.md Rounds 1–3): completing workflows with fabricated data, condensing 800-word observations to 150, injecting restrictive qualifiers into predicate questions.
Same root pathology, two vocabularies: YLO calls it 'context poisoning'; POEM calls it 'interpretation / fabrication'.
Governance scar: Oscar + Alex exist only in the client's .claude/commands/ — not in POEM core (poem-001 blocker, severity high). The field improvements have no sync path home.

convergence.title: Where all three agree
shared: Same .hbs Handlebars prompt templates with {{placeholder}} injection
Same five step types — transform / checkpoint / action / gate / elicit
Same declared inputs[] / outputs (stores) name-matched dataflow contract
Same 'human gates forward decisions, not chatter' discipline
convergence.note: Agreement is total on lenses 1 + 2 (prompts + workflow shape). Divergence is total on lens 3 (architecture).

items: Co-located .json output schemas — validate worker writes mechanically instead of hoping the worker returns the right shape (YLO's single biggest gap).
A preflight / validate pass (validate-workflow.yaml) — catch type mismatches and dangling refs before any step runs.
A modelType alias layer ("analysis"/"quick" → concrete slug) — would have prevented the flux-schnell phantom-model bug (open loop #1).

cross_pollination.direction: POEM / supportsignal should steal from YLO
items: Append-only EAV audit store — every attempt, error, and human decision preserved (a compliance asset for the NDIS / safety-regulated domain, not just a nicety).
Resumability from the store — re-read, skip done steps, append a fresh attempt.
Structural isolation for the Oscar-as-LLM path — replace 600 lines of 'don't summarise' rules with subagent isolation, where the rules become unnecessary.

verdict.headline: YLO is a working prototype of what POEM's Oscar was trying to be

alignment.detail: ~90% shared already — both descend from AWB Gen 3. POEM YAML ↔ YLO JSON is mechanically convertible. They could share one schema.
Not a tweak — a choice of which POEM runtime survives: in-session conductor (YLO) vs external compiled CLI (poem-executor).

verdict.recommendation: If POEM commits to in-session execution, adopt YLO's conductor as POEM's runtime and demote poem-executor to the headless / CI lane. The probes have already de-risked it.
